title: A Guide to Submission of Income Tax Return
author: Md. Mobarock Hossain ACA
author_bio: Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB).
summary: This article gives individual taxpayers in Bangladesh a practical overview of income tax return filing, including who must file, the applicable tax-free thresholds under the Finance Act 2022, the November 30 filing timeline, extension options, penalties for late filing, and cautionary notes for wealth statements and non-resident taxpayers.

highlights: The article explains when an individual in Bangladesh is required to submit an income tax return.
It summarizes the tax-free income thresholds described in the Finance Act 2022 for general taxpayers and special categories.
It notes that November 30 is the regular filing deadline for individual assessees, with limited extension options.
It outlines the consequences of late filing, including fines, delay interest, and concealment penalties.
It advises taxpayers to preserve acknowledgements and supporting tax records because files may be reopened later.

sections.heading: Who Must File
points: Individuals exceeding the applicable non-taxable income threshold are required to submit a return.
Some persons must file regardless of income, including certain TIN holders and persons holding specified assets or business registrations.

sections.heading: Thresholds and Taxpayer Categories
points: The article references the Finance Act 2022 thresholds for general taxpayers.
It separately notes higher tax-free limits for women, third-gender taxpayers, senior citizens, persons with disabilities, and war-wounded freedom fighters.

sections.heading: Filing Timeline
points: November 30 is identified as the main filing deadline for individual assessees in Bangladesh.
The write-up mentions extension options and cautions taxpayers not to wait until the final day.

sections.heading: Penalties and Practical Risks
points: Late filing may trigger fines, delay interest, and additional penalties depending on the facts.
The article warns taxpayers to complete wealth statements carefully and maintain supporting records for future review.

sections.heading: Non-resident Context
points: The article briefly discusses the treatment of non-resident taxpayers and when filing may not be mandatory.
It notes that non-residents are taxed on Bangladesh-source income.

notes: This entry was structured from searchable ICAB journal PDF snippets rather than the publication API.
